findings.summary: prevention-only: already-gitlinked brains are neither detected nor healed, and the PR body was silent on it. Disclosed in the body; detection deliberately held for the follow-up doctor PR
the in-repo pi path now commits nothing and signals nothing (pi-extension.ts:153 discards the refusal); not data loss per ADR-0064 journal, but the body omitted the behaviour change. Disclosed
vfkb save exits 0 on refusal so a script cannot distinguish it from success; documented as deliberate, with SaveResult.refused as the programmatic discriminator
the CLI hint read $VFKB_DATA_DIR and printed the wrong path when unset; now uses the engine-resolved brain from r.message
docs/RUNBOOK:34 still listed vfkb save as reading git state for the in-repo brain it describes, where it is now always a refusal
a fully gitignored in-repo brain is now versioned nowhere; confirmed out-of-design (init never ignores .vfkb wholesale) and the guard does not consult check-ignore
brain == repo root classifies standalone and would git add -A the operator's tree; pre-existing, out of scope, flagged so it is not read as closed
saveAndPush re-shells isStandaloneBrain after save already computed it; harmless duplication

ruledOut: the defect reproduces against main and is closed at HEAD, both directions observed
the proof can fail: 7 of 11 tests go red against main's git.ts; micro-reverts isolate each guard; no test asserts on an exit code
standalone path intact: 487/487 incl. guardrails.test.ts, vfkb save on a standalone brain, and the L4 docker image verified LIVE
saveAndPush all four (committed, standalone) combinations traced against a real bare remote; no path where a standalone brain silently stops pushing
SaveResult.refused is additive/optional; the only consumers anywhere are cli.ts and pi-extension.ts
ADR-0064 journal semantics unaffected (pairsAtHead keys off dirname(brain), unchanged before and after)
edge cases (nonexistent brain, dangling symlink, git absent, unreadable parent, bare repo, worktree, .git-as-file) behave identically to pre-fix
